title: NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND - 211info
text: NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals achieve independence and equality by providing information and referral, employment information, literature, scholarship programs, and a blindness-related adaptive equipment store. The Free White Cane Program provides rigid fiberglass canes. 200 E Wells Street Baltimore, MD 21230 Eligibility: -Primarily blind and visually impaired individuals, but sighted persons may also join -Free White Cane Program: Must be blind or visually impaired, must be requesting the cane for personal use, and it must have been more than 6 months since a previous white cane request Hours: Varies by chapter National office: Monday-Friday 5am-2pm Email (541) 653-9153 -. Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 653-9153 Oregon Chapter [email protected] Main phone (206) 604-5507 Washington Chapter [email protected] Main phone (410) 659-9314 National Office-Free Canes [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Call or visit website for more information -Free White Cane Program: Visit website to complete an online application or to print and mail a completed paper application FEES: -Varies by service -Free White Cane Program: None DOCUMENTS: Call for information AREA SERVED: Nationwide Services: Blind Mobility Aids Disability Rights Groups * Blindness Specialized Information and Referral * Blindness Student Financial Aid * Blindness If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND
